title: Untrusted Inputs, Integrity, Privacy, Release, Rollback, and Defense · Program Defense
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for untrusted inputs, integrity, privacy, release, rollback, and defense and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit limitations, and named browser, filesystem, terminal, packaging, operating-system, load, security, and release transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for production Bookbot release and residual-risk defense. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case pra…
objectives: Threat-model paths, devices, links, huge lines, malformed encodings, control characters, output injection, resource exhaustion, and race limits.
Keep reports and logs to authorized metrics and safe identifiers, with explicit excerpt, retention, deletion, and sharing policy.
Use a modern digest for content identity while distinguishing accidental-corruption evidence from authenticated provenance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"
